about_formats: PDF is a fixed-layout document format often used for sharing, printing, and archiving.
DOCX is an editable Word document format designed for rewriting, formatting, and collaborative editing.
common_problems: Scanned PDFs may not be editable
tips: Use higher resolution scans for OCR accuracy

faq.question: Can I convert PDF files into editable DOCX documents?
faq.answer: Yes. This tool converts PDF files into editable DOCX documents so you can revise and format them in Microsoft Word or similar apps.

faq.question: Will the document structure be preserved?
faq.answer: The conversion aims to preserve document structure, including text blocks and layout, as closely as possible.

faq.question: Is the output suitable for Word editing?
faq.answer: Yes. The DOCX output is designed to be editable in common word processors and easier to revise than a locked PDF.

faq.question: Is the conversion secure and online?
faq.answer: Yes. The process is secure, fast, and works online without requiring any software installation.

faq.question: How do I get the converted document?
faq.answer: After conversion finishes, you can download the editable DOCX file immediately.
